key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep learning model can jointly predict laser welding penetration state, penetration depth, and weld seam morphology from weld pool images and process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables in-situ quality control for laser penetration welding by turning camera-based monitoring into actionable defect and geometry predictions during manufacturing.
method: Uses CMOS camera images of the top weld pool plus welding parameters as inputs.
Combines convolutional neural networks with state-space models to capture spatial and temporal welding features.
Employs a carefully constructed dataset to improve robustness and generalization.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% accuracy for weld cross-section reconstruction.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the captured weld-pool dataset, so transfer to new materials, optics, or welding regimes should be validated carefully.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
